Ivana Rasch
Featured · M.S. Thesis · NYU Steinhardt · 2025

DJing in Latent Space

A custom MIDI DJ controller wired directly to a 512-dimensional MusicVAE latent space, with output to a Yamaha Disklavier acoustic piano. Physical gestures — knobs, magnetic encoders, proximity sensors, a capacitive touch strip — navigate and interpolate between musical ideas in real time.

The project asks: what new forms of musical agency open up when you design both the hardware and the model? Starting from a survey of real-time neural audio style transfer approaches, the work pivoted to the MIDI domain where latent space navigation became physically intuitive.

The controller features a dual-deck layout with 14 potentiometers, encoders, and sensors. A Python/pretty_midi pipeline extracts song chunks. Web MIDI routes input to a browser-based interpolation engine, with 11-step spherical interpolation on the crossfader and attribute vector arithmetic for note density and interval control.

MusicVAEMagentaTeensy 4.1KiCad PCBWeb MIDIAS5600VL53L0XTrill BarDisklavierpretty_midi
RoleDesigner, Engineer, Researcher
AdvisorDr. Magdalena Fuentes
HardwareTeensy 4.1, KiCad PCBs, AS5600, VL53L0X, Trill Bar
OutputYamaha DKC500RXG Disklavier
Latent space512-dim MusicVAE
Year2025
Ivana Rasch
Interactive Installation · Intersecting Narratives · Analog Dream, Berlin · 2023

Kinetic

Kinetic invited the public into a dynamic realm where motion, light, and sound seamlessly converged. Each movement created a symphony of visual and auditory elements, transforming gestures into captivating patterns of light and harmonized sound.

A Pure Data patch captured real-time body movement data from a Kinect sensor, translating audience motion into data-driven sound and light automation. The piece invited visitors to become performers without realizing it — their movement through the space shaped the sonic and visual environment around them. Over 80 people interacted with the installation across its run.

The work uses gestural control to redefine interactive art experiences, in a spirit similar to artists like Rashaad Newsome, who also bridge technology and art to build immersive environments. It shares a throughline with the thesis: physical gesture as interface, the body as controller, real-time data as compositional material.

Pure DataKinectReal-timeSound designBody trackingGestural controlInteractive installation
RoleSoftware Developer, Sound Designer
VenueAnalog Dream, Friedrichstraße
LocationBerlin, DE
DatesJun 2023 – Aug 2023
Visitors80+
ToolsPure Data, Kinect sensor

Ivana Rasch
Research · Spatial Audio · NYU · 2024

Sound Event Detection + Direction of Arrival

📡

A multi-task SELD model for 4-channel First Order Ambisonics audio. STFT features computed across all channels, with Direction of Arrival estimated in 3D Cartesian coordinates. Built for CS-GY 9223 Machine Listening (Prof. Juan Pablo Bello), based on DCASE 2019 Task 3.

The model jointly learns to detect sound events and estimate their spatial position, requiring simultaneous reasoning across temporal and spatial dimensions.

FOASTFTPyTorchDCASE 2019Spatial audioMulti-task learning
CourseCS-GY 9223 Machine Listening
InstructorProf. Juan Pablo Bello
BenchmarkDCASE 2019 Task 3
Input4-ch First Order Ambisonics
Year2024
Ivana Rasch
Research · ML · DCASE 2023 Task 6A

Automated Audio Captioning

A dual-encoder architecture for automated audio captioning on Clotho v2, combining ConvNeXt and BEATs audio encoders with a GPT-2 decoder fine-tuned via LoRA. Best configuration (r=16, α=32, dropout=0.1) achieved SPIDEr 0.243.

Built with teammates Yujin and Mehmet Atil. Responsibilities included the SPIDEr-FL metric section, result interpretation, dependency resolution, and implementing the ClothoWithEmbeddings dataset class.

ConvNeXtBEATsGPT-2LoRAClotho v2DCASE 2023
TaskDCASE 2023 Task 6A — AAC
DatasetClotho v2
Best SPIDEr0.243
ArchitectureConvNeXt + BEATs → GPT-2 + LoRA
Year2024
Ivana Rasch
Research · Survey · 2024

Neural Audio Style Transfer for Live Performance

🌀

A systematic evaluation of Magenta RealTime, AFTER, RAVE, and DDSP for live performance contexts. Each system benchmarked on latency, timbral control, and playability.

The survey produced an honest scholarly mapping of each approach's constraints — revealing that real-time audio style transfer wasn't yet viable for expressive, low-latency physical performance. This was the direct pivot point toward the MIDI latent space approach in the thesis.

RAVEDDSPAFTERMagenta RealTimeReal-time audioLive performance
TypeLiterature survey + evaluation
SystemsMagenta RT, AFTER, RAVE, DDSP
ContextM.S. Thesis pre-study
Year2024
Ivana Rasch
Research · Interactive Installation · Katzen Arts Center · 2023

Linguistic Melodies

Linguistic Melodies explores the intersection of speech analysis, music generation, and interactive art through capacitive touch sensing, audio feature extraction, and sound synthesis in Pure Data. The piece was motivated by a multilingual, multicultural background — speech recordings and painted portraits of participants from a range of cultural backgrounds speaking different languages, extending a traditional canvas into an intermedia installation.

Twelve participants answered a 22-question survey twice — once in English, once in their native language — across languages including Spanish, German, French, Italian, Lithuanian, Vietnamese, Urdu, Portuguese, Catalan, Arabic, and Finnish. Recordings were segmented into silent, voiced, and unvoiced regions using a semi-supervised technique in MATLAB, and audio features determined a melodic sequence reflecting the emotional and tonal character of each speaker's voice.

A photo of each participant was converted into a stencil and painted onto canvas in wavy lines colored by their flag, connected by a hand-painted line of conductive paint. A capacitive touch interface — conductive paint, copper foil, and a Teensy 3.2 running an MPR121 sensor — let visitors trigger a participant's speech, melody, or a blend of both by touching their portrait. Playback was spatialized across a 4-channel surround system at the Katzen Arts Center, with recently activated content constantly recirculating to keep the piece's texture evolving.

Pure DataCapacitive touchConductive paintMPR121Teensy 3.2Speech analysis4-channel surroundGenerative music
RoleConcept, Hardware, Sound Design
VenueKatzen Arts Center
Participants12 speakers, 11 languages
HardwareTeensy 3.2, MPR121, conductive paint
Playback4-channel surround sound
Year2023
Ivana Rasch
Hardware · Sampler / Mixer · 2024

Entropy Mixer

A custom hardware sampler and mixer that takes any external audio input and lets you manipulate it live through a bank of faders, potentiometers, and a 4x4 pad, all routed through a custom PCB.

Its signature feature is a gating effect driven by an entropy pattern, inspired by Laurie Spiegel's algorithmic approach to composition — the further a knob is turned, the more entropy is introduced into the gate, pushing the sound from tightly rhythmic gating into increasingly unpredictable, chaotic territory.

Custom PCBSamplerEntropy gateLive audio inputHardware mixer
RoleDesigner, Engineer
FunctionSampler + live audio mixer
Signature featureEntropy-driven gate, after Laurie Spiegel
Year2024
Ivana Rasch
Research · ISMIR Late-Breaking Demo · 2025

Bringing Global Music to Magenta RealTime

DJs shape energy in real time, yet current controllers remain track-based. Generative models and neural style transfer enable style blending, but real-time systems are rare, and Magenta RealTime offers low-latency generation while being trained mainly on Western music. This work fine-tunes Magenta RT on curated playlists to create a culturally adaptive, generative DJ instrument.

An 88-minute playlist of Indian music was curated and segmented into training examples, with a modified data loader built to respect melodic and rhythmic cycles rather than arbitrary chunks. The fine-tuning process reverse-engineered and aligned Magenta RT's preprocessing pipeline to the new dataset.

Training loss dropped sharply from ~25,000 to near zero within the first 5,000 steps, stabilizing with no signs of overfitting, while accuracy rose from 20% to over 99%. In informal listening tests, the fine-tuned model reproduced raga pitch contours, tala rhythms, and timbral textures far more clearly than the baseline. The work points toward real-time, content-preserving style transfer and harmony alignment, with generative models like Magenta RT evolving into instruments DJs can actively shape rather than just trigger.

Magenta RealTimeFine-tuningGenerative DJIndian classical musicStyle transfer
AuthorIvana Rasch Chinchilla
Dept.Music Technology, NYU Steinhardt
VenueISMIR Late-Breaking Demo
Dataset88-min curated Indian music playlist
Best accuracy99%+ (from 20% baseline)
Year2025
Ivana Rasch
Software / Hardware · Pure Data · 2024

Face Disklavier

A Pure Data patch that lets a face control a Disklavier — using FaceOSC to track facial landmarks from a webcam feed in real time, then mapping expressions directly into performance parameters.

FaceOSC extracts features like eyebrow height (left and right independently), mouth height and width, and head pose/orientation, sending them over OSC at 60fps. Inside Pure Data, each feature is scaled and routed into the control chain, translating raised eyebrows, an opening mouth, or a head tilt into expressive control over the instrument.

The result turns facial gesture into a playable interface for the Disklavier, extending the same idea running through the other builds here: the body as controller, and physical or expressive gesture as compositional input.

Pure DataFaceOSCDisklavierComputer visionGestural control
RoleDesigner, Engineer
InputFaceOSC (webcam face tracking)
Tracked featuresEyebrows, mouth height/width, head pose
OutputYamaha Disklavier
Year2024
